inline_comments.message: You first need to register a node before you can retrieve the modules for that node however CSIT passes... it is just asserting a 200 which would happen but there would be no modules yet. Switch these tests around and perhaps assert some info on a returned module to ensure it works.

Actually this is the continuation of https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/cps/ncmp-dmi-plugin/+/127687

Setup related code is present here.
https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/cps/ncmp-dmi-plugin/+/127687/14/csit/plans/dmi/setup.sh

Were you looking for this? Or something else?

You need to register a node first and only then as part of the sync are the modules added for that cm handle. Fetching the modules needs to be done after it. I would also suggest you check for an expected returned module as done in other tests like:

Get modules for registered data node
  ...
    ${responseJson}=     Set Variable       ${response.json()}
    Should Be Equal As Strings              ${response.status_code}   200
    FOR   ${item}   IN  @{responseJson}
            IF   "${item['moduleName']}" == "stores"
                Should Be Equal As Strings              "${item['revision']}"   "2020-09-15"
            END
        END
See here for more information on model sync: https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/CPS+User+Story+Demos (#8)

Actually in the CSITs we are just testing the integration points and not looking at the returned data.
We are just relying on the response codes , as long as it is 2XX we are good that the integration is fine and would work. So I have explicitly added it in the description just to eliminate confusion.
